title: The King Enrolled Among Us
subtitle: A decree goes out, and the Lord of eternity is counted in the census of Caesar

stillness: Be still, and let the wars within you fall silent.
For the eternal Word is not born except in a heart at peace.
His place is made in peace.
gospel: In those days, while Mary was still with child,
a decree went out from Caesar Augustus
that the whole world should be enrolled.
And all went to be enrolled, each into his own city,
from which they drew their origin.
Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth,
into Judea, into the city of David which is called Bethlehem,
because he was of the house and family of David,
to be enrolled together with Mary, his espoused wife, who was with child.
behold: Attend to each thing as if you were present.
See the Lord, for your sake, enrolled by name in the census upon the earth,
that your name might be enrolled in the heavens.
In this He gave the example of perfect humility,
which the Saviour begins at His birth and carries on even to His death.
Consider also that, though the blessed Virgin had already conceived the King of heaven and earth,
yet she, together with her spouse Joseph, willed to obey the imperial decree.
See the Lady labor again upon this long road:
from Nazareth to Jerusalem are thirty-five miles,
and Bethlehem lies beyond, on the slope of the mountain, toward the south.
Yet though she was with child and near to her delivery,
she was not weighed down in body,
for, as Augustine says, being a Virgin with child, she leapt with a wholesome lightness.
The Light that she bore within her could have no weight.
consider: Behold how He came in the flesh who would enroll His chosen ones in eternity.
To this our King we owe the tribute of faith and of justice,
to be professed in heart, in mouth, and in deed.
We owe Him the coin: the soul stamped with the light of His countenance.
As no man was exempt from the coin,
so no man is exempt from the keeping of the commandments.
Not lightly is so great and so gracious a humility to be gazed upon:
that He deigned not only to be made flesh for us,
but to be made flesh at the very time
when, newborn, He would be enrolled in the census of Caesar,
and for our deliverance would put Himself under servitude.
He is the King of peace, the Prince of peace;
fitting it was that before His birth He should send peace ahead, as a herald.
And He willed to be born when the world was at peace,
for that peace was His servant.
prayer: would make me, a wretched one, to be among Your chosen,
that You might enroll me in eternity.
To You, our King, let me profess the tribute of faith and of justice,
in heart, in mouth, and in deed.

latinJewel.latin: Verbum æternum non nascitur, nisi in corde pacato; unde Psalmus dicit: Factus est in pace locus ejus.
latinJewel.english: The eternal Word is not born except in a heart at peace; whence the Psalm says: His place is made in peace.
